constraint_form: inequality-constrained domains.
guarantee_basis: stochastic process design; numerical discretization and score-model approximation remain practical considerations.
tradeoff: domain-aware diffusion is more specialized than unconstrained Euclidean diffusion.

constraint_form: constrained support.
guarantee_basis: reflection mechanism; exact assumptions need method-section check.
tradeoff: implementation and guarantees depend on the supported domain/reflection construction.

constraint_form: manifold constraints.
guarantee_basis: manifold-valued diffusion construction.
tradeoff: handles manifold constraints, not arbitrary inequality systems without a manifold formulation.

constraint_form: manifold constraints.
guarantee_basis: Riemannian SGM formulation.
tradeoff: requires a known or modeled manifold structure.

constraint_form: convex constrained sets.
guarantee_basis: valid mirror map and dual-primal transformation.
inference_dependency: mirror map and reverse diffusion sampler.
tradeoff: practical scope depends on tractable mirror maps for the target domain.

constraint_form: convex and nonconvex constraints in the paper's experiments.
guarantee_basis: projection/constrained optimization during sampling.
inference_dependency: projection or constrained solve during sampling.
tradeoff: stronger constraint handling adds optimization cost and depends on solver quality.

constraint_form: terminal hard constraints for molecular conformations.
guarantee_basis: trajectory optimization solve/surrogate quality.
inference_dependency: trajectory optimization or MPC-style constrained sampler.
tradeoff: hard terminal control increases sampling complexity.

constraint_form: constraints on generated distribution statistics or properties.
guarantee_basis: dual optimization/training objective.
inference_dependency: standard diffusion sampling after constrained training.
tradeoff: distributional constraints do not automatically imply every individual generated sample is feasible.

constraint_form: arbitrary compact convex sets in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: bijective gauge map and bounded distribution-approximation analysis.
inference_dependency: gauge-map evaluation around the flow-matching sampler.
tradeoff: applicability depends on constructing a valid gauge map for the target compact convex set.

constraint_form: general possibly nonconvex constraints with differentiable distance to the constraint set.
guarantee_basis: learned approximate map; feasibility is map-validity dependent rather than exact by construction in this first-pass reading.
inference_dependency: learned mirror map/inverse around the diffusion sampler.
tradeoff: broader constraint coverage comes with approximation error and the need to validate the learned map.

constraint_form: convex polytope support constraints.
guarantee_basis: polytope-to-ball homeomorphism or barycentric-coordinate construction.
tradeoff: requires a valid polytope representation and map construction.

method_family: simplex-Euclidean bijection for categorical flow matching.
constraint_form: simplex support for categorical/discrete data modeling.
guarantee_basis: smooth simplex-Euclidean bijection and exact discrete recovery construction.
tradeoff: final validity for downstream combinatorial objects depends on the decoding assumptions.

method_family: Fisher-geometric flow matching for discrete data.
constraint_form: categorical simplex/positive-orthant support induced by discrete data distributions.
guarantee_basis: Fisher-Rao manifold construction and geodesic flow matching.
inference_dependency: manifold flow sampler and discrete decoding.
tradeoff: support geometry is respected in the categorical distribution space; task-level validity after decoding still needs evaluation.

constraint_form: arbitrary compact convex sets, with extensions to star-convex and geodesic-convex sets.
guarantee_basis: bijective gauge construction, bounded distribution-approximation analysis, and stated nonconvex extensions.
inference_dependency: gauge map, ball-domain sampler operations, and inverse map.
tradeoff: constraint coverage depends on a valid gauge/bijective construction for the target geometry.

method_family: constrained diffusion warm-start for trajectory optimization.
constraint_form: trajectory dynamics and safety constraints in nonconvex robotics settings.
guarantee_basis: numerical solver verification; the diffusion model alone is a guided warm-start distribution.
inference_dependency: diffusion sampling followed by numerical trajectory optimization.
tradeoff: improves speed by providing high-quality initial guesses, but final guarantees depend on the downstream solver.

constraint_form: constrained-domain support constraints.
guarantee_basis: reflected dynamics and boundary constraint construction.
tradeoff: support preservation depends on boundary handling and numerical simulation error.

constraint_form: bounded-domain support constraints.
guarantee_basis: reflected SDE construction and boundary conditions.
tradeoff: requires valid reflected dynamics/boundary treatment for the target domain.

constraint_form: convex and nonconvex constraints in the paper's experiments.
guarantee_basis: valid discretization of reflected Brownian motion and accept-reject sampling.
tradeoff: efficiency and sample quality depend on proposal behavior, acceptance, and mixing.

constraint_form: trajectory and safety constraints, including discrete control barrier functions.
guarantee_basis: constrained Langevin iterations and CBF constraints; final strength depends on iteration/tolerance choices.
inference_dependency: iterative constrained sampling during reverse diffusion.
tradeoff: avoids retraining but adds iterative optimization cost at sampling time.

constraint_form: nonconvex equality and inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: landing mechanism in constrained stochastic dynamics.
tradeoff: feasibility and efficiency depend on the landing construction and the cost of constrained dynamics.

constraint_form: bridge-form constraints that can be manually specified and combined.
guarantee_basis: mathematical validity of the manual-bridge construction and constraint-combination rules.
tradeoff: requires constraints to be expressible through the manual-bridge framework.

constraint_form: time-series hard constraints, including many simultaneous constraints in experiments.
guarantee_basis: projection step applied during posterior sampling; tolerance and projection quality matter.
tradeoff: repeated projection improves feasibility but adds sampling-time correction cost.

constraint_form: bonding, stereochemical, geometric, motif, and pocket-design constraints in protein design.
guarantee_basis: proximal feasibility updates and ADMM decomposition in the generative process.
inference_dependency: feasibility-update and ADMM correction steps during sampling.
tradeoff: domain-specific constraint handling increases sampling/correction complexity.

constraint_form: arbitrary constraint sets in the paper's stated stable-diffusion framework, with convex-constraint guarantees discussed.
guarantee_basis: proximal Langevin correction and projection/surrogate constraint evaluation.
inference_dependency: iterative latent correction during sampling; surrogate or simulator if constraints are not directly differentiable.
tradeoff: training-free and compatible with stable diffusion, but correction quality depends on decoder gradients and constraint/surrogate fidelity.

constraint_form: proxy-constraint functions used during diffusion inference.
guarantee_basis: trust-region/proxy-guidance heuristic; first-pass source does not support a hard pointwise guarantee.
inference_dependency: multiple proxy-gradient steps and manifold trust checks during sampling.
tradeoff: flexible and training-free, but guidance strength and trust criteria affect sample quality and feasibility.

constraint_form: trajectory feasibility as adherence to the data/manifold of valid offline trajectories.
guarantee_basis: local manifold approximation and projection; feasibility is data-manifold dependent.
inference_dependency: nearest-neighbor retrieval, local PCA, and projection at sampling steps.
tradeoff: training-free but adds retrieval/projection cost and depends on offline dataset coverage.

constraint_form: measurement-space residual constraints for inverse problems.
guarantee_basis: hard residual projection plus local descent/backtracking analysis.
inference_dependency: fixed-budget correction iterations using operator evaluations and autodiff primitives.
tradeoff: efficient for inverse problems, but guarantees are local/per-step and tied to the measurement model and residual budget.

constraint_form: sequence-level constraints and logic rules over discrete outputs.
guarantee_basis: sampling-time differentiable constraint optimization.
inference_dependency: constrained discrete diffusion correction during sampling.
tradeoff: training-free constraint insertion adds sampling-time optimization and depends on the constraint optimizer.

constraint_form: manifold/geometric support constraints.
guarantee_basis: manifold-valued flow construction.
inference_dependency: geometry-aware flow sampler and geometric computations.
tradeoff: support modeling depends on the availability and cost of geometry-specific kernels or spectral computations.

constraint_form: hard constraints in systems where gradients are sparse or expensive, including PDE examples.
guarantee_basis: correction step in the ECI sampling loop.
inference_dependency: ECI correction during each iterative sampling step.
tradeoff: avoids gradients/fine-tuning but adds correction stages and depends on the available constraint-correction routine.

constraint_form: constraints handled by projection-based constrained sampling.
guarantee_basis: training-sampling alignment plus projection-based inference; pointwise feasibility comes from the projection procedure, not the loss alone.
tradeoff: improves alignment but requires training with the intended projection mechanism.

constraint_form: measurement consistency and data-manifold constraints for inverse problems.
guarantee_basis: correction term analysis and empirical validation; not a standalone hard feasibility guarantee.
tradeoff: simple to attach, but feasibility depends on the base solver and correction quality.

constraint_form: terminal constraints/manifold constraints.
guarantee_basis: optimal-control analysis; as penalty vanishes, the terminal law approaches a generalized Wasserstein projection onto the constraint manifold.
tradeoff: adds optimal-control guidance cost and relies on terminal constraint geometry.

constraint_form: arbitrary constraints in the paper's inference framework, including linear and nonlinear examples.
guarantee_basis: optimization-style correction; first-pass evidence does not support an exact hard feasibility claim for all constraints.
tradeoff: faster than denoiser-backprop approaches, but correction accuracy and constraint satisfaction remain task-dependent.

constraint_form: linear output constraints or residual-distribution constraints for noisy inverse problems.
guarantee_basis: constrained DDIM update construction.
tradeoff: scope is linear inverse constraints; broader validity depends on reducing the problem to this structure.

constraint_form: nonlinear equality and inequality constraints.
guarantee_basis: exponential convergence in 2-Wasserstein distance under stated regularity assumptions.
tradeoff: rigorous guarantees require the paper's regularity assumptions and sampler discretization choices.

constraint_form: bounded, unbounded, continuous, discrete, categorical, ordinal, and product-domain constraints in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: bridge construction with domain-enforcing singular force.
tradeoff: broad domain support depends on constructing the appropriate bridge forces for the target domain.

constraint_form: domain boundary constraints.
guarantee_basis: reflected Langevin dynamics and boundary-condition analysis.
inference_dependency: constrained Langevin sampler with boundary handling.
tradeoff: requires boundary-condition modeling and specialized numerical sampling.

constraint_form: arbitrary nonlinear physical constraints in the paper's stated scope.
guarantee_basis: physics-based correction step applied during sampling.
tradeoff: zero-shot and flexible, but correction cost and exactness depend on the physical constraint evaluator.

constraint_form: control barrier function safety constraints for planning.
guarantee_basis: barrier certificate proving forward invariance of a robust safe set and finite-time convergence.
tradeoff: provides certification for executed paths but adds a correction QP and depends on valid CBF/safe-set modeling.

constraint_form: PDE residual/physics constraints used as soft inductive bias.
guarantee_basis: architecture-level soft inductive bias.
tradeoff: deliberately avoids strict hard constraints to tolerate model misspecification.

constraint_form: inverse-problem observation constraints handled by the underlying diffusion inverse algorithm.
guarantee_basis: extrapolation over the base algorithm's approximation structure; not a separate hard feasibility mechanism.
inference_dependency: LLE extrapolation on top of an existing inverse sampler.
tradeoff: improves efficiency/performance but inherits feasibility behavior from the underlying inverse algorithm.
